popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description: Níall of Carrick (also Neil) was the second man to bear the title Mormaer, or Earl, of Carrick. He was successor of mormaer Donnchadh of Carrick. He may have been Donnchadh's son, or else as suggested …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick於1202年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾卡里克時，他的父親Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick28歲，母親Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick24歲。他於1234年與Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick在蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1256年6月13日在蘇格蘭阿吉爾卡里克享年54歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick于1202年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾卡里克时，他的父亲Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick28岁，母亲Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick24岁。他于1234年与Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick在苏格兰艾斯爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1256年6月13日在苏格兰阿吉爾卡里克享年54岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Carrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): habitational name from Carrick, a steep and rocky district in Ayrshire, named in Old Welsh with carreg ‘rock’, borrowed into Gaelic as carraig.

History: The earliest bearers of the Scottish Carrick surname (see 1 above) were descendants of the 12th-century lords of Galloway who became earls of Carrick in the early 13th century. The earldom passed by marriage to the   Bruce   family in later in the 13th century and then to the crown with the succession of Robert Bruce, earl of Carrick, to the throne in 1306. Illegitimate descendants of the earliest earls, themselves noblemen, continued to use the Carrick surname.

variants.name: McCarrick
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Con Charraige ‘son of Cú Charraige’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the rock’.
Anglicized form of Mac Con Chathrach ‘son of Cú Chathrach’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the fort’.

variants.name: Corrick
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Carrick or Carrack, a habitational name probably from Castle Carrock (Cumbria), which is earlier recorded as Castel Carok. The placename derives from British castello- ‘castle, camp’ (a borrowing from Latin castellum ‘fort’) + a word related to Welsh caerog ‘fortified’.

variants.name: Karrick
meanings: Possibly also a shortened and altered form of Irish   McCarrick  . Compare   Carrick   2.
Americanized form of German Karrich: metonymic occupational name for a carter or cartwright, from Middle High German karrech ‘cart’, from Latin carruca. The surname Karrick is very rare in Germany.

nameMeanings.name: Niall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of disputed derivation; it may mean ‘cloud’, ‘passionate’, or perhaps ‘champion’. It was adopted by the Scandinavians in the form Njal and soon became very popular among them. From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, it has been strongly revived among non-Gaelic speakers since the latter part of the 20th century.

variants.name: Neale
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

en.lifeSummary: When Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick was born in 1202, in Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick, was 28 and his mother, Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick, was 24. He married Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick in 1234, in Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 13 June 1256, in Carrick, Argyll, Scotland, at the age of 54, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick est né en 1202, à Carrick, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick en 1234, à Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 13 juin 1256, à Carrick, Argyll, Écosse, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick 1202 in Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick 28 und seine Mutter Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick 24. Er hat 1234 in Ayrshire, Scotland Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Juni 1256 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Carrick, Argyll, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick è nato il nel 1202 nel Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick nel 1234, nel Ayrshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 13 giugno 1256, nel Carrick, Argyll, Scotland, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrickが1202年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアカーリックで生まれた時，父Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrickは28歳，母Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrickは24歳であった。1234年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアでMargaret Stewart Countess of Carrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1256年6月13日にスコットランドアルグイルカーリックで54歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick(이)가 1202년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 카릭에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1234년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1256년 6월 13일 스컷랜드 아그이 카릭에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick nasceu em 1202, em Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick em 1234, em Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de junho de 1256, em Carrick, Argyll, Scotland, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick родился в 1202 г. в местности каррикк, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick в 1234 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 13 июня 1256 г. в местности каррикк, аргилл, Шотландия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Niall de Carrick 2nd Earl of Carrick nació en 1202, en Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Donnchadh Mac Gilbert 1st Earl of Carrick, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Avelina FitzAlan Countess of Carrick, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Margaret Stewart Countess of Carrick en 1234, en Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 13 de junio de 1256, en Carrick, Argyll, Scotland, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
